evals.prompt: What's the first step to making a new astrodb?
evals.expected_output: Explains that the first step is to create your own GitHub repository from the astrodb-template (via 'Use this template' on https://github.com/astrodbtoolkit/astrodb-template-db) and provide its URL, and asks for a name for the database. Does not ask for or mention a data file — that comes in a later step.

assertions.text: Instructs the user to create a new GitHub repository from the astrodb-template (e.g. 'Use this template' on astrodb-template-db) and to provide the new repo's URL
Asks the user for a database name
Does not ask for or reference a data file, parsing, or ingestion (that is a later step)

evals.prompt: I made my repo from the template, it's at https://github.com/jdoe/bd-survey-db . Call the database BdSurvey and get it set up.
evals.expected_output: Clones https://github.com/jdoe/bd-survey-db into the working directory, verifies the cloned repo has the template structure (a data/ directory, a database.toml, and a schema.yaml), removes the template's pre-generated schema files (schema_erd.png and docs/schema/*.md), updates database.toml so db_name is 'BdSurvey', creates an artifacts/ directory, and creates an artifacts/directions.md file (populated or as a template). Does not handle a data file.

assertions.text: Clones the provided repo URL (https://github.com/jdoe/bd-survey-db) into the working directory
Verifies the cloned repo has the template directory structure: a data/ directory, a database.toml file, and a schema.yaml file
Updates database.toml so db_name is set to the user-provided name (BdSurvey)
Removes schema_erd.png from the cloned repo
Removes the .md files in docs/schema/ from the cloned repo
Creates an artifacts/ directory inside the cloned repo
Creates artifacts/directions.md (either populated from user input or as a template to fill in)
Does not ask for or handle a data file

evals.prompt: Set up a new astrodb database called NearbyGalaxies.
evals.expected_output: Notes the database name (NearbyGalaxies) and, because no repo URL was given, asks the user to create a repo from the astrodb-template ('Use this template') and provide its URL before cloning. Does not invent a repo URL or fabricate a clone.

assertions.text: Asks the user to create a repo from the astrodb-template and provide its URL before cloning
Does not invent a repo URL or clone an arbitrary repository
Does not ask for or reference a data file
